sheet.description: Cursed Cannon, as the name says, allows you to unleash blasts of cursed energy. As simple as it is, its power is undeniable.

description.p: Starting at 1st level, your CE output is off the charts, and thanks to your technique allowing you to output it in a destructive death ray. As an action, you can spend 1 cursed energy to release a beam of cursed energy. Make a ranged cursed energy attack roll with a range of 150/600 feet. On a hit, it deals 3d8 necrotic damage.
Beginning at 3rd level, the damage increases to 6d8, to 8d8 at 6th, to 10d8 at 11th, to 12d8 at 18th level.

sheet.description: For 1 CE, make a ranged CE attack roll with a range of 150/600 feet. On a hit, it deals {{granite:blast:damage}}d8 necrotic damage.

description.p: At 3rd level, your versatility with your blasts has increased. For 1 extra cursed energy, you can split your Granite Blast into however many blasts as you would like, with damage dice spread evenly between blasts.

sheet.description: For 1 extra CE, you can split your Granite Blast into however many blasts as you would like, with damage dice spread evenly between blasts.

description.p: Beginning at 6th level, your shots are malleable and extremely difficult to avoid. Once per attack, you can choose to spend up to 5 cursed energy points to add to the attack roll.

sheet.description: Once per attack, you can choose to spend up to 5 CE to add to the attack roll.

description.p: Once you reach 11th level, you have managed the peak of Granite Blast. As a Full Turn Action (Action + Bonus Action + Movement), you can charge up the blast for 4 cursed energy to release on your next turn as an action. After charging and releasing on your next turn, Granite Blast becomes a 150 ft cone, forcing creatures in it to make a Dexterity Save against your Cursed Energy DC, on a failure the creature takes your Granite Blast damage plus an additional amount of dice equal to your Cursed Energy modifier.

sheet.description: You can charge Granite Blast for 4 CE to release on your next turn as an action. On release, Granite Blast becomes a 150 ft cone, forcing creatures in it to make a Dexterity Save against your Cursed Energy DC. On a failure the creature takes your Granite Blast damage plus {{ce:modifier}} dice.

description.p: Finally, at 18th level, you have honed your technique to the maximum, gaining your domain expansion. While inside it, at Initiative 20, you can choose up to three targets to suffer the full effects of Granite Blast.

sheet.description: At Initiative 20, you can choose up to three targets to suffer the full effects of Granite Blast.
